metadata.description: Persistent record of /pipeline-audit rounds — structural audits of the magpie-agent's machinery (modules docs, validators, instruction surface, cross-doc consistency, efficiency). Behavioral answer-quality lives in validation_rounds.json; this file is its structural counterpart.
metadata.instructions: Append new rounds to the 'rounds' array. Each round: round number, date, commit, lenses run, total findings, severity breakdown, per-lens breakdown, corroborated findings, root-cause clusters, report file path, guards added, status. The detailed findings report (severity-ordered with evidence) lives in audit/pipeline_audit_roundN.md, referenced from each round entry.

corroborated.finding: Hoist underdelivered — AGENT.md shrank only 10 lines (-7%) while verifiers.md adds 1,888 words; broad 'module' trigger means net token cost per session went UP; CRITICAL WARNINGS section in AGENT.md still duplicates MANDATEs 7, 8, 16

Realization-aware verification missing — 13/46 module docs have stale or fabricated 'Verified Against' footers; Module 18 describes non-default realization as active (Critical); realization regex misses 20/59 non-month-format names; my own freshly-written MANDATE 8 example fabricates fbask_jul23

root_cause_clusters.candidate_intervention: Add single Python check that for each module doc: extract Realization header + Verified Against footer; cross-reference against config/default.cfg; verify directory exists. ~80 LOC. Closes 13 footer bugs + Module 18 Critical + MANDATE 8 fabrication + future drift.

Extend check_gams_citations_impl.py: (a) parse end-of-range :NN-MM; (b) verify content match within +/-5 lines of cited line; (c) WARN on bare-basename ambiguity with realization candidates. ~150 LOC. Pattern 12 has zero coverage today.

Doc-editing session: (a) drop 'module' and 'equation' from verifiers.md triggers; (b) delete CRITICAL WARNINGS duplicates; (c) fix MANDATE 8 fabrication (fbask_jul23 -> fbask_jan16); (d) replace hardcoded /Users/turnip path; (e) reconcile MANDATE 16 name; (f) update validate-semantic.md schema 1.1 note; (g) update audit/README.md directory listing; (h) mark each MANDATE Verified by: <script> | human.
New checkers: check_param_defaults.py (Pattern 13), check_citation_content.py (Pattern 12), check_unbacktiked_gams.py. Extend GAMS-index regex for c<N>_/cm_/sm_. Generalize Check 1 to all 46 modules.

scripts/refresh_aggregate_counts.py — read canonical values from validation_rounds.json.cumulative_stats + wc -w; update well-defined placeholders. ~50 LOC. Or simpler: drop precise numbers from prose; cite cumulative_stats.
Python pass: recompute consumer counts via grep -l; update Module_Dependencies.md, module_10.md, module_11.md, modification_safety_guide.md.
Python checker: for each vm_*/pm_* reference in a module doc, look up declared dimensions in producer module's declarations.gms; flag mismatches.
Hand fixes for biome count, c60_biodem_level default, vm_water_cost unit, climate-type count, region count. Overlaps Cluster 4 (Pattern 13 coverage) and Cluster 5 (count drift).

recurring_classes_for_mechanization: Citation content mismatch / line drift (10+ instances)
rounds.status: triaged; fix+mechanize deferred to follow-up session (per user scope choice)
rounds.notes: First R1 audit caught a confabulation introduced in this session: MANDATE 8 worked example in agent/helpers/verifiers.md claims livestock default is fbask_jul23; actual default is fbask_jan16; fbask_jul23 does not exist in the repo. This is exactly the failure mode the lens-based audit exists to catch — the audit detected the agent inventing a worked example for the rule warning against inventing realization names. Recommended ordering for follow-up: address Clusters 1, 3, 5 first (root fixes that close ~40 of 71 findings). Reserve Clusters 2, 4, 6, 7 for a second follow-up session (higher effort).

rounds.summary: User authorized continuing into fix phase after R1 triage. Executed 7 fix rounds covering 4 of 8 R1 clusters via root interventions; 1 mechanization (realization-validity validator); deferred Clusters 4 (validator coverage gaps) and 7 (interface-attribution scan beyond R1 spot fixes) for future sessions.

cluster_1_realization_validity.mechanization: scripts/check_module_realizations.py (~150 LOC) — cross-references each module doc's Realization header + Verified Against footer against config/default.cfg AND verifies directory exists. Hooked in as Check 19 of validate_consistency.sh.
cluster_1_realization_validity.doc_fixes: 18 module-doc footer fabrications fixed (modules 09, 17, 20, 28, 31, 34, 36, 40, 44, 45, 50, 51, 53, 55, 56, 57, 58, 59); Module 18 (CRITICAL: described non-default flexcluster_jul23 as active) — added prominent Quick Reference for default flexreg_apr16 with correct 9 equations + regional dimension; full body rewrite flagged as pending.
cluster_1_realization_validity.result: Validator surfaces 0 errors / 0 warnings on this check; previously hidden bugs now mechanically guarded.
cluster_1_realization_validity.self_finding_resolved: MANDATE 8 worked example fbask_jul23 → fbask_jan16 (actual default); ledger entry updated; croparea_nov24 added as a different verified-absent example.

cluster_2_citation_drift.mechanization: scripts/check_gams_citations_impl.py extended (~50 LOC added) — now parses range END :NN-MM (was start only), warns on bare-basename ambiguity, performs content-match check within ±5 lines of cited line when a backticked GAMS identifier is nearby (Pattern 12 coverage).
cluster_2_citation_drift.doc_fixes: Module 13 input.gms 6 citations re-derived (off by 35-45 lines); equation citations q13_tau / q13_tau_consv corrected; vm_tau cluster-vs-supreg internal contradiction resolved; full-path prefix applied to all bare-basename citations in module_13.md.
cluster_2_citation_drift.bulk_clamp: Auto-clamped 31 +1 range-end overruns and 18 larger range-end overruns to file EOF (all real drifts surfaced by the enhanced checker; previously hidden by 'only check start of range' bug).
cluster_2_citation_drift.advisory_state: 874 bare-basename ambiguity warnings and 25 content-mismatch advisories now surface non-blockingly (informational, not failing).

guards_added: scripts/check_gams_citations_impl.py: silent-fallback fixed — when full-path realization doesn't exist, emit FILE warning instead of silently rewriting via bare-basename walk-order (closes Cluster 5 HIGH)
scripts/check_module_realizations.py: HEADER_REAL_RE loosened to accept **Realization:** (colon inside bold) + parenthetical-in-h1 form; VERIFIED_RE prefix made optional to catch no-dot footers
scripts/check_gams_variables.py: regex extended to cover ic<N>_, oq<N>_, ov<N>_ prefixes (closes Cluster 5 MED FN gap)
scripts/refresh_aggregate_counts.py: added validator_main_checks (20), validator_sub_checks (35), bug_taxonomy_patterns (14) marker keys to MARKER_VALUES (closes Cluster 6)
rounds.status: triaged + fixed + verified (this session); root-cause-cluster commits stacked; full body rewrites of M30/M38/M80 deferred to future sessions
rounds.notes: R3 surfaced 82 findings vs R1's 71 — more raw, but THIS IS EXPECTED per [[feedback_confabulation_relocates]]. R2 closed 4 surfaces (header-realization drift, simple citation drift, hoist incomplete, aggregate-count drift in audit docs); bugs relocated to: (a) body-level realization drift in M38/M30/M80 — Check 19 only catches header drift, so body citation drift surfaced unguarded; (b) fabricated identifier templates in module §17.2-style 'overview' sections (14 invented names in M11 — Cluster 2 NEW); (c) dependency-count drift in cross-cutting docs that R2 only spot-fixed (Cluster 4 RECURRING); (d) FP/FN in newly-added validators (Cluster 5 NEW — guard surface moved as predicted). CRITICAL count rose from 1 to 3 — not because the agent regressed, but because confabulation moved to higher-stakes surfaces (default-realization mismatches in M38/M73 mislead modification-safety reasoning). Strongest next mechanization candidate: check_doc_var_existence.py (single new validator closes Cluster 2 entirely). Status of this session's fixes: 18 of 20 CRITICAL+HIGH findings fixed directly; 2 deferred (M30+M80 body rewrites need their own session); ~12 MEDIUM mechanical fixes applied; LOW findings documented but not all fixed.

corroborated.finding: Shadow-table sync gap — Module_Dependencies.md L268 says '25 modules' for M11 but L139 (R3 recount) says 27; modification_safety_guide.md Appendix B (L1075-1080) has 6 consumer counts inflated by +1 (R3 fixed §1.2 only).

Trigger overlap (R3 finding persists) — 'default realization' and 'modify code' each appear as trigger keywords in TWO helpers, causing redundant ~2000-token loads on common queries; 'scenario' substring fires 5 helpers.

M11 attribution drift across multiple sections — R3 fixed §17.2 (CRITICAL fabrication cluster) but §3 retained 6 'Module X or Module Y' rows; worst case vm_p_fert_costs attributed to M50/M51 but actual is M54.

recurring_classes_for_mechanization: Shadow-table sync gap (R3 → R4): when primary tables are recomputed, secondary tables in other files remain stale. Candidate: generic dep-count checker scanning ALL tables.

Wrong consumer attribution tables (R1 → R3 → R4): chronic. M09 + M11 + M29 + Module_Dependencies + safety_guide all manifest the same pattern. Candidate: for every backticked vm_*/pm_*/im_*, verify producer/consumer claim against grep.
Validator coverage gaps in new scopes (R3 → R4): Check 18 dormant; helpers/commands unscanned. Candidate: periodic regex-coverage audit with format-variant test cases.
Multi-section consistency within one doc (NEW in R4): M14 pcm_tau. Candidate: cross-section identifier-consistency check.
guards_added: scripts/check_default_realizations.py: relaxed Pattern C regex to accept the canonical **Default Realization**: form (was dormant for all 46 module docs)
scripts/check_doc_var_existence.py: SCAN_DIRS extended to include agent/helpers/ and agent/commands/ (closes coverage gap that hid the s22→c22 bug)
rounds.status: triaged + most CRITICAL/HIGH fixed in single commit (d0870ae); ~14 LOW findings deferred to audit/next_session_plan.md
rounds.notes: R4 produced 55 findings vs R3's 82 — a 33% reduction. CRITICAL count dropped from 3 to 0 — the R3-followup mechanizations (check_doc_var_existence + body-citation extension) closed the M11-§17.2-fabrication and M38-body-drift classes entirely. NO new CRITICAL emerged. This REVERSES the R1→R3 confabulation-relocation pattern — instead of bugs moving to new surfaces, the surfaces themselves narrowed and total found bugs dropped. Where R4 bugs concentrate: (a) cross-cutting doc consumer/attribution tables (M09 §5.2, M11 §3, Module_Dependencies shadow tables) — 'hand-maintained denormalizations' of grep-derivable facts that don't sync when primary tables are recomputed; (b) validator coverage gaps that R3-followup didn't close (Check 18 was effectively dormant for the canonical doc format!); (c) numerical and citation drift in less-trafficked sections. Strongest R5 candidate: generic consumer-attribution checker that recomputes counts from grep and flags mismatches across ALL tables (primary + shadow). This single mechanization would close Clusters 3 + 4 + 5 entirely. Validator state after fixes: 35/36 passed, 1 known advisory (s59 unit-conv FP); validator wall-clock dropped 8.78s → 6.34s (-28%). Validator coverage now spans modules/ (Check 14) + cross_module/ + core_docs/ + agent/helpers/ + agent/commands/ (Check 21). Check 18 actually operational for the first time on canonical doc format.

rounds.status: triaged; fix + mechanize pending user review
rounds.notes: R5 ran immediately after a high-change session (commits d44823f magpie4 scaffolding + 327116c /feedback removal + 1c7df77 feedback->audit rename + e7bbb39 G3/G4 regression questions). Lens 4 (instruction-surface) deliberately biased toward the new surface; AGENT.md / deployed copies / helper trigger sync all verified clean. The session's changes themselves introduced no CRITICAL bugs; the sed-sweep produced LOW-severity collateral in historical narrative (Cluster D). Most HIGH/MEDIUM findings predate this session (Pattern 6 count drift, Pattern 10 citations, M18 wrong consumer, M30 default realization deferred since R3). Highest-leverage intervention: one-line scope extension in check_gams_citations_impl.py (Cluster B) — closes the largest historical bug class outside its current modules/-only scope. Six lenses ran in parallel; total wall-clock ~13 minutes; ~6 Opus 4.7 subagents.
